question.question_stem:
题目：2.1 For a nitrate ion selective electrode a calibration curve as shown below was obtained using a series of standard nitrate solutions containing $0.5\ \mathrm{mol,dm^{-3}}$ $\mathrm{K_2SO_4}$, $1.0	imes10^{-3}\ \mathrm{mol,dm^{-3}}$ $\mathrm{H_2SO_4}$ and $2.6	imes10^{-3}\ \mathrm{mol,dm^{-3}}$ $\mathrm{Cl^-}$ ion as the background. Decide whether it is feasible to measure concentration $\mathrm{NO_3^-}$ in serum under the above conditions.

2.2 Given are selective coefficients of $\mathrm{Cl^-}$, $\mathrm{SO_4^{2-}}$ and $\mathrm{ClO_4^-}$ versus $\mathrm{NO_3^-}$ as follow: $K_{\mathrm{NO_3^-},\mathrm{Cl^-}} = [\mathrm{NO_3^-}] / [\mathrm{Cl^-}] = 4.9	imes10^{-4}$, $K_{\mathrm{NO_3^-},\mathrm{SO_4^{2-}}} = [\mathrm{NO_3^-}] / [\mathrm{SO_4^{2-}}]^{1/2} = 4.1	imes10^{-3}$, $K_{\mathrm{NO_3^-},\mathrm{ClO_4^-}}} = [\mathrm{NO_3^-}] / [\mathrm{ClO_4^-}] = 1.0	imes10^{-3}$, where the units of the concentrations are in $\mathrm{mol,dm^{-3}}$. Which is the best to reduce the interference of $\mathrm{Cl^-}$ to $\mathrm{NO_3^-}$ determination, so as to control the error in the $\mathrm{NO_3^-}$ concentration within 1 %, when there are $1.40	imes10^{-3}\ \mathrm{mol,dm^{-3}}$ $\mathrm{NO_3^-}$ and $1.60	imes10^{-2}\ \mathrm{mol,dm^{-3}}$ $\mathrm{Cl^-}$ in serum: (a) $\mathrm{AgNO_3}$ (b) $\mathrm{Ag_2SO_4}$ (c) $\mathrm{AgClO_4}$ Calculate the amount of the salt that should be added to 1 $\mathrm{dm^3}$ of the sample solution to be measured.

2.3 The $\mathrm{NO_3^-}$ ion concentration was determined by this method at 298 K. For 25.00 $\mathrm{cm^3}$ sample solution the electronic potential, E, is measured to be –160 mV. After adding 1.00 $\mathrm{cm^3}$ $1.00	imes10^{-3}\ \mathrm{mol,dm^{-3}}$ $\mathrm{NO_3^-}$ standard solution to the above solution, E changes to –130 mV. Find the pNO₃ of the serum.

2.4 The selective coefficient of $\mathrm{CH_3COO^-}$ versus $\mathrm{NO_3^-}$ $K_{\mathrm{NO_3^-},\mathrm{CH_3COO^-}}} = 2.7	imes10^{-3}$. If $\mathrm{AgCH_3COO}$ instead of $\mathrm{Ag_2SO_4}$ is added to the sample solution of question 2.2, find the upper limit of the pH value below which the same requirement in question 2.2 can be met. $K_{sp}(\mathrm{AgCl}) = 3.2	imes10^{-10}$, $K_{sp}(\mathrm{Ag_2SO_4}) = 8.0	imes10^{-5}$, $K_{sp}(\mathrm{AgCH_3COO}) = 8.0	imes10^{-3}$, $K_a(\mathrm{CH_3COOH}) = 2.2	imes10^{-5}$, $A_r(\mathrm{N}) = 14.00$
question.question: Find the upper limit of the pH value.
